codeReview.summary: The feature adds cache-backed thread restoration and disconnected-state UI, but it does not satisfy the relaunch and reconnect requirements in production: storage is still process-local only, and live-stream disconnects never trigger automatic catch-up.

issues.description: `appSecureStoreProvider` still returns `InMemorySecureStore`, so trusted-session state, selected-thread state, and cached threads disappear as soon as the app process dies. That means the claimed offline relaunch/selected-thread restoration path cannot work on a real app relaunch, even though VAL-THREAD-010 / VAL-CROSS-007 require cached content and selected-thread restoration after relaunch. The added integration test only reuses the same in-memory repository instance (`apps/mobile/integration_test/reconnect_offline_cache_test.dart:18-40`) instead of exercising persisted storage across process restart.

When the websocket drops, `_handleLiveStreamDisconnected()` only marks the UI stale/offline; it never calls `_scheduleReconnectCatchUp()`. After a mid-session disconnect/background resume, the thread stays disconnected until the user manually taps Retry, so automatic reconnect/catch-up is missing for VAL-THREAD-010 / VAL-CROSS-003. The new test coverage also models this as a manual recovery path (`apps/mobile/test/features/threads/thread_detail_page_test.dart:506-511`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission docs say mobile secrets should live in platform secure storage and discovery recommends `flutter_secure_storage` plus a real local cache, but the library does not call out that the mobile secure-store provider is still wired to an in-memory placeholder. That mismatch makes persistence-dependent features easy to over-claim.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: codex-mobile-companion-discovery.md:446-452 recommends `flutter_secure_storage` and a real cache; .factory/library/environment.md:17 says sensitive material belongs in platform secure storage; apps/mobile/lib/foundation/storage/secure_store_provider.dart:4-5 still returns `InMemorySecureStore()`.

summary: Fail: reconnect/offline UX is partially implemented, but real relaunch persistence is absent and live disconnects do not automatically resubscribe/catch up. I wrote the review report to `.factory/validation/mobile-app/scrutiny/reviews/integration-reconnect-catchup-and-offline-cache.json`.
